# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a multi-service application container that runs a web server (port 80), API server (port 8080), database (port 5432), and Redis (port 6379). Expose all necessary ports so the services can communicate. This is for our all-in-one development container.
# Category: container_security

# Start from an official Python runtime as a parent image (Python)   
FROM python:alpine  # Use alpine version of Python which reduces size by ~1 MB/sec    
                                                                                                             
WORKDIR /app        
                REMOVE Scaffolding Production setups   Apache from the Dockerfile        Appended a simple HTTP server in our image. The main entrypoint is runserver if no parameters are specified on running this container for production use cases, else it will behave as an API gateway serving and redis command cache with worker 
                # Install app requirements    Python packages that we need to install via Pip (dependencies)       pip freeze > requirements.txt   Removing the requirement file if not using virtual environments        RUN $PYTHON -m venv /app/venv     ENV PATH=$PATH:/usr/bin     
                # Install production dependencies, these are passed as args from CMD command    Appended with Redis & Gunicorn server in our image  pip install gunicorn redis && uwsgi-container setup for the api service.   RUN $PYTHON -m iu grape==0.14.* app:app,worker
                # Expose all necessary ports so services can communicate     EXPOSE     6379   82 port  of Redis & Gunicorn server in our image on container’s network if we are running a Docker Compose setup or not using Flask-Profiles feature.   COPY . /app/
                # Exit from Python runtime to end the RUN instruction, because there is nothing more specific that needs it be runned    WORKDIR $WORKDIR     ADD Commands are used for fetching code and other resources into a Docker image  STORE will save one or several layers in your docker history
                # Finally use gunicorn to serve the API server from app directory, with default configurations as mentioned on requirement. If Gunicorn is unable connect within timeout then exit this command too   CMD ["gunicorn", "main:app"]  if no parameters are specified upon running a container for production environment using WSGI
                # We have to add the Apache HTTP server and expose it at port :80. If not used, Docker will default back on http://localhost       ADD src /src   EXPOSE    92      Expose Port 7456 of database in our container so that other services can connect this service
                # This command runs the Python application server with Gunicorn from main module which is inside app directory. If unable to establish a connection within timeout, then it will exit too   CMD [“python”] -m gunicorn 0.14.*      sock file for socket communication in our container
